FooterHelp.title: How to use TrustLedger
FooterHelp.intro: Use this guide when you need the fastest path through contract creation, dashboard navigation, and dispute resolution.
FooterHelp.stepCreateBody: Open New Contract, choose whether you are the client or freelancer, add the counterparty wallet, amount, timeline, and document link, then send the offer.
FooterHelp.stepDashboardBody: Use Dashboard to fund accepted offers, submit work, approve delivery, open disputes, claim deadlines, and rate the other party.
FooterHelp.stepJurorBody: Disputed contracts link into arbitration. Jurors use the Juror page to register stake and the dispute page to commit and reveal votes.

Home.subtext: TrustLedger holds your ETH in a smart contract escrow. Funds release on client approval - or via decentralized juror arbitration if there's a dispute.
Home.featureEscrowDesc: ETH is locked in the contract. Neither party can move it unilaterally.
Home.featureArbitrationDesc: Staked jurors vote on outcomes via a commit-reveal system. Minority voters are slashed.
Home.featureWarrantyDesc: Optionally retain 5-15% of payment until a warranty period elapses.

Dashboard.connectWallet: Connect your wallet to see your contracts.
Dashboard.title: Dashboard
Dashboard.emptyDescription: TrustLedger contracts appear here after both wallets agree on terms and escrow is funded or ready to fund.
Dashboard.emptyStepCreate: Create a contract with the other party's wallet address, payment amount, deadline, and document link.
Dashboard.emptyStepFund: The client funds escrow after the offer is accepted, so neither party controls the money alone.
Dashboard.emptyStepReview: Track delivery, approve work, or open a dispute from the contract card.
Dashboard.walkthroughIntro: TrustLedger holds freelance payment in escrow until work is approved, or a dispute is resolved by jurors.
Dashboard.walkthroughStepCreateBody: Add the counterparty wallet, amount, timeline, and a contract document link. The other party reviews before work starts.
Dashboard.walkthroughStepTrackBody: Dashboard cards show funding, delivery, approval, deadlines, documents, and any action your wallet can take.
Dashboard.walkthroughStepResolveBody: If work is disputed, the card links to arbitration so evidence, votes, and payouts stay tied to the same contract.
Dashboard.workSubmitted: Work submitted — waiting for client approval.

Create.connectWallet: Connect your wallet to propose a contract.
Create.subtitleClient: You propose the terms. The freelancer reviews and accepts, then you fund the escrow to start the project.
Create.subtitleFreelancer: You propose the terms; the client reviews and locks the {token} in escrow on acceptance. Funds are released once you deliver and the client approves — or a dispute is resolved.
Create.bewareWarning: BEWARE: The proposed currency is locked on submit AND freelancer agreement!
Create.usdcNotSupported: USDC not supported on this network.
Create.freelancerAddressHint: The wallet that will review, accept, and deliver the work.
Create.clientAddressHint: The wallet that will review, fund, and approve this contract.
Create.freelancerEmailHint: A signed magic link will be sent here so the freelancer can review and accept via the web.
Create.clientEmailHint: A signed magic link will be sent here so the client can review and accept via the web.
Create.escrowHintClientUsdc: Total USDC you agree to lock in escrow once the freelancer accepts. You will approve the USDC transfer when funding.
Create.escrowHintClientEth: Total ETH you agree to lock in escrow once the freelancer accepts.
Create.escrowHintFreelancerUsdc: Total USDC the client will lock in escrow on acceptance. The client must approve the USDC transfer before funding.
Create.escrowHintFreelancerEth: Total ETH the client will lock in escrow on acceptance.
Create.contractDocumentUriHint: IPFS link or URL to the off-chain agreement. A hash of this is stored on-chain.
Create.clickToBrowse: Click to browse or drop a file
Create.passphraseHint: Share this with the other party via a separate secure channel. You cannot decrypt without it.
Create.pinataJwtHint: Required to upload the document to IPFS.
Create.arweaveBackupTitle: Permanent backup to Arweave (optional - for long-term legal retention)
Create.arweaveUploadFailed: Arweave upload failed - check wallet balance.
Create.estimatedDurationHint: How long the project should take.
Create.bufferFactorHint: Project deadline = duration × buffer / 1000. E.g. 1200 = 1.2× buffer.
Create.acceptanceWindowHint: How long you have to review submitted work. Minimum 2 days.
Create.arbitrationFeeHint: Percentage of escrow set aside for jurors if a dispute is opened (0-50%).
Create.warrantyHoldbackHint: Portion withheld from the freelancer until the warranty period elapses.
Create.warrantyPeriodHint: How long the hold-back is locked after work is approved.
Create.usdcApprovalNote: You will need to approve the escrow contract to spend your USDC before funding.
Create.freelancerWillReview: The freelancer will review and accept. You will then be prompted to fund the escrow to start the project.
Create.failedToSendMagicLink: Failed to send magic link - check server env vars.
Create.previewSubtitle: This preview updates as you edit. Nothing is deployed until you review and confirm.
Create.draftSavedNote: Draft terms are saved locally in this browser so you can keep editing before deployment.
Create.confirmSendQuestion: Are you sure you want to send this to the {role}?
Create.confirmSendDescription: After you confirm, your wallet will open and the contract proposal will be submitted on-chain. Keep editing if any term is wrong.

Juror.connectWallet: Connect your wallet to manage your juror registration.
Juror.title: Juror Panel
Juror.subtitle: Stake ETH to become eligible for dispute committees. Earn fees for majority votes.
Juror.stakeLockedFor7Days: Stake is locked for 7 days after deposit. Eligible after {date}.
Juror.reputationBelowMin: Reputation below minimum (20). Minority votes reduce reputation by 10 each.
Juror.registerSubtitle: Stake at least 0.01 ETH. Stake is locked 7 days before you become eligible. Minority votes slash 10% of your stake.
Juror.registered: Registered. Stake is locked for {days} days before eligibility activates.

Reputation.title: Reputation
Reputation.subtitle: Look up cumulative escrow ratings from completed TrustLedger contracts.
Reputation.connectToLookUp: Connect to look up your own address quickly.
Reputation.recoveryProgressValue: {progress} / 3 toward clearing {pending} low {pending, plural, one {rating} other {ratings}}
Reputation.scoresNote: Scores are submitted by contract parties after completion (1-100). Dispute resolutions may apply automatic penalties. Receiving 3 scores ≥ 70 after a low score (≤ 30) earns a +50 recovery bonus.
Reputation.failedToRead: Failed to read reputation. Check that you are on the correct network.
Reputation.registryNotConfigured: Reputation is not available for this TrustLedger deployment yet.
Reputation.trustLedgerNotConfiguredWithHint: TrustLedger is not configured for {network}. Switch your wallet to {networks}, or set this network's NEXT_PUBLIC_* contract address variables and redeploy.
Reputation.trustLedgerNotConfiguredNoHint: TrustLedger is not configured for {network}. Set the NEXT_PUBLIC_* contract address variables for this network and redeploy.
Reputation.registryLookupError: Could not discover the reputation registry for this network. Check that your wallet is connected to a supported TrustLedger network.

Arbitration.youAreSelected: You are selected - commit your vote
Arbitration.bondRequired: Bond required: <amountStrong>{amount} ETH</amountStrong> (1.5× fee pool). Returned if you win; forfeited if you lose.
Arbitration.appealWindowElapsed: Appeal window elapsed - ruling can be executed.
Arbitration.notPartyOrJuror: You are not a party or juror in this dispute.
Arbitration.commitInstructions: Choose a completion percentage (0 = fully refund client, 100 = fully pay freelancer). A random salt will be generated and saved locally - do not clear your browser storage before revealing.
Arbitration.saltSaved: Your salt and completion % have been saved to localStorage. You will need them to reveal.
Arbitration.revealInstructions: Enter the same percentage and salt you committed with. These are pre-filled from localStorage if available.
Arbitration.saltInvalid: Salt must be a 0x-prefixed 32-byte hex string (66 characters).

Client.reviewTerms: Review the terms and document below. Accepting locks {amount} in escrow.
Client.usdcNote: This contract uses USDC. You will approve the transfer in a separate step before funding.
Client.notAwaiting: Contract is not awaiting acceptance. It may already be accepted, rejected, or withdrawn.
Client.escrowFunded: The escrow is funded and the project deadline timer has started.
Client.proposalDeclined: The proposal has been declined. No funds were transferred.
Client.wrongWalletDesc: This link is for {address}. Please switch to that account.
Client.connectWalletPrompt: Connect the wallet at {address} to review contract #{id}.

Freelancer.clientProposes: The client proposes {amount} in escrow. Review the offer below. Accepting signals your agreement — the client will then fund the escrow to start the project deadline.
Freelancer.clientWillFund: The client will now be notified to fund the escrow. The project deadline starts once funds are locked.
Freelancer.offerHasBeenDeclined: The offer has been declined. No funds were held.
Freelancer.contractNotAwaiting: This contract is not awaiting your acceptance (current: {status}). It may already be accepted, rejected, or withdrawn.
Freelancer.wrongWalletDesc: This link is for {address}. Please switch to that account.
Freelancer.connectWalletPrompt: Connect the wallet at {address} to review contract #{id}.

Common.connectedAs: Connected as {address}. Click to open wallet options.

Decrypt.title: Decrypt AES-256-GCM document
Decrypt.decryptionFailed: Decryption failed — wrong passphrase or corrupted bundle.
